replies.msg: 無事既無事既

I’ve been to Tibet. It’s famous for its breathtaking scenery at high altitude. It’s a glamorous holiday destination. However if you will have altitude sickness such as dizziness, headaches and trouble sleeping, then bring some medicine 💊along. In fact my friend who’s just been to Mount Everest base camp ⛺️ sent me his photos by which I was awestruck. The shape of Mount Everest is sharply defined against the starry nights by which you’ll be awestruck. A tour of Mount Everest would be a once in a lifetime experience. It’s a fair bet that you go to Tibet for the summer holiday because Yuan has fallen a bit over the last couple months. I would say Tibet is also the best place for stargazing 🔭 as it’s much closer to the heavens than any other places on earth 🌍 It has some of the highest lakes in the world which are rich with fish 🐡🐠🐟and other marine life🐬🐳🐋🦈🦑🦐🦞🦀and the surrounding countrysides are inhabited by flocks of yellow ducks 🦆 as well as wild horses 🐎 and donkeys. The lakes in Tibet such as Lake Paiku which is four thousand five hundred and ninety two metres above sea level reflects the beauty of the stars and the nearby snow mountains 🏔 giving a double view of the starry sky and snow mountains ⛰ at night. You can set up a tent 🏕 and camp there to get the best view of the stars 🌟 and galaxies 🌌 Again, going on a trip to Tibet this summer is a safe bet since the Yuan has dropped a little. Remember to take loads and loads of pictures. 😊
